title: clinopodium macranthum
intro: Clinopodium macranthum, also known as large-leaved gypsywort or large-leaved clinopodium, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to the mountainous regions of China, Sichuan, and Yunnan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Clinopodium macranthum is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and sometimes combined with other herbs in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules.
